commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.2: the deck — a flip-stack of letterpress specimen cards (#25)

Reskin the Forms gallery's `deck` node (scrollytelling-inception/index.html)
into a stack of print-specimen PLATES that wears the gallery's shared
type-specimen vocabulary while keeping its OWN distinct mechanic.

Each step-card is a matte plate framed with jade registration crop marks
(a single .dk-card::before pseudo painting 8 strips) + a tracked mono
catalogue label, no bloom — echoing the cascade title's specimen frame
(aj1.7.1). Its distinct demo mechanic: each of the three plates is set in a
DIFFERENT real type family (grotesque / mono / serif), so the stack performs
the movement's thesis 'the same idea, wearing many faces' — same structural
step, three faces — without duplicating cascade's glyph-audition.

- Honest readout: the catalogue label derives wholly from real structure —
  plate index/total + the actual font-family name. Nothing fabricated.
- paint(p,n) stays the pure scroll-driven 3D lift (no timers/WAAPI); the
  face per plate is static identity, so nothing needs REDUCE_MOTION gating
  beyond the global keyframe kill.
- Fixed a silent fallback: the serif family string carries double quotes,
  which broke an inline style="..." attribute and left the serif plate on
  the default face. Family is now set in JS alongside textContent.

Verified in Chrome across the deck's full scroll range: cards lift cleanly
01 grotesque -> 02 mono -> 03 serif terminal; no page console errors.
